home.tagline: Join us for our Madagascar celebration
home.joinUs: Do you want to join us?
home.introText: As you might know, we have friends and family in different parts of the world. Therefore, next to our intimate celebration in Belgium, we will also celebrate in Imerintsiatosika, Madagascar on 14 November this year (only one to two hours away from the capital, Antananarivo).
home.rsvpText: Are you interested in flying to Madagascar in November to celebrate with us? We would love to see you there. Please RSVP as soon as possible so we can count you in and keep you updated.
home.tripInfoIntro: On this page you can access some extra information to help you prepare for your trip if you are a first time traveler to Madagascar.
home.accommodationText: Book yourself an accommodation in Antananarivo for the duration of the wedding (13–15th November). We can arrange the transfer to the wedding location for you. There are multiple options, depending on your budget and comfort needs.
home.accommodationTransfer: Transfer to the wedding location in Imerintsiatosika will be arranged
home.accommodationOptions: Multiple accommodation options available at various price points
home.travelText: Do you want to travel before or after the wedding? We know a local tour operator that can organize a tour with you and the other guests, depending on your travel wishes and length of stay.
home.deadlineText: Please let us know by 15 March if you would like to make use of our contact. Tours and accommodation for November are booked out quickly.
home.tourOperator: A trusted local tour operator is available to help with planning
home.tourWithGuests: Possibility to join organized tours with other wedding guests
home.bookQuickly: Tours and accommodation book out quickly — plan ahead!
home.newsletterSubtitle: Subscribe to receive updates about our Madagascar celebration
home.subscribeSuccess: Thank you! You'll receive updates soon.
home.subscribeError: Please enter a valid email address.
home.questionsText: Feel free to reach out to us anytime

planYourTrip.paragraph1: Madagascar is a beautiful country, so you might want to consider doing a tour before or after the wedding date. Antananarivo, the capital, will be a great starting point.
planYourTrip.paragraph2: For your safety, we advise against travelling all by yourself. Booking a tour with a trustworthy tour operator and an experienced guide is the safest option.
planYourTrip.paragraph3: Some accommodations book out quickly for November, so don't wait too long. We're happy to help you get started.
planYourTrip.inspirationText: Need some first inspiration? Here some amazing travel tips:
planYourTrip.card1Bullet1: Antananarivo, the capital, is the ideal starting point
planYourTrip.card1Bullet2: Plan a tour before or after the wedding on November 14
planYourTrip.card2Bullet1: Book with a trustworthy local tour operator
planYourTrip.card2Bullet2: An experienced guide helps with the language barrier
planYourTrip.card2Bullet3: Enjoy a stress-free and unforgettable experience
planYourTrip.card3DeadlineText: Let us know before 15 March if you'd like us to connect you with a local tour operator.
planYourTrip.regionNorthDescription: Where turquoise lagoons meet ancient forests. Drift between the islands of the Nosy Be archipelago — where whale sharks glide through warm waters — then head inland to the otherworldly red spires of Tsingy Rouge and the orchid-filled trails of Montagne d'Ambre. This is Madagascar at its most tropical and cinematic.
planYourTrip.regionWestDescription: A landscape that feels borrowed from another planet. Walk among 800-year-old baobab trees as the sunset turns the Avenue of the Baobabs into pure gold, then venture into the razor-sharp limestone cathedral of Tsingy de Bemaraha, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Nearby Kirindy hides the world's smallest primate, the Pygmy Mouse Lemur.
planYourTrip.regionCenterDescription: The soul of Madagascar lives here. Journey to the sacred UNESCO hill of Ambohimanga, the spiritual heart of the Merina kingdom, while rolling green hills stitched with terraced rice paddies stretch in every direction. It is in this timeless landscape, at Imerintsiatosika, that we will celebrate our wedding.
planYourTrip.regionEastDescription: Wake to the haunting call of the Indri — the world's largest living lemur — echoing through misty rainforest canopy in Andasibe, Madagascar's most rewarding wildlife park. Then glide along the Pangalanes Canal past palm-fringed fishing villages before reaching the vanilla coast, which supplies over half the world's vanilla.
planYourTrip.regionSouthDescription: Follow the legendary Route Nationale 7 south until Madagascar's 'Grand Canyon' appears: Isalo National Park, with its soaring sandstone canyons, hidden waterfalls, and jewel-toned natural pools. End your journey where the spiny forest meets the sea at Ifaty — a surreal, cactus-studded coast giving way to warm, laid-back beaches.
planYourTrip.defaultHoverText: Hover over a region on the map to discover what it has to offer
planYourTrip.mapAlt: Illustrated map of Madagascar showing North, East, West, and South regions
